question.text: Offences under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances Act, 1985 are listed under which part of the PMLA Schedule?

answer.text: They are not included in the PMLA Schedule

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Offences under the Narcotic Drugs and Psychotropic Substances (NDPS) Act, 1985 are listed under Part A, Paragraph 2 of the PMLA Schedule. Part A offences have no monetary threshold — any NDPS offence generating proceeds of crime qualifies for ML prosecution. NDPS offences also attract enhanced punishment (up to 10 years instead of 7).
**The Trap:** Part B covers Customs Act offences (with a monetary threshold). Part C covers cross-border implications of Part A offences. NDPS Act offences are firmly in Part A as serious predicate crimes.
**Cross-Exam Context:** NDPS Act offences were the original focus of international AML efforts (Vienna Convention 1988). Their placement in Part A (no threshold) reflects their severity in the global AML framework.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Part A, Paragraph 2 lists specific NDPS sections including trafficking in poppy straw, coca, opium, cannabis, manufactured drugs, psychotropic substances, and financing illicit traffic. These are among the most serious scheduled offences.
